notes: 200 patients (1:1) randomised to caffeinated coffee (~1 cup/day) vs caffeine abstinence post-cardioversion. Published HR 0.61 (95% CI 0.42-0.89) is caffeinated vs abstinence: caffeinated had 39% LOWER AF recurrence. The trial was powered in the OPPOSITE direction: expecting a 41% REDUCTION in AF with ABSTINENCE (caffeine is historically viewed as proarrhythmic). In the caff/abst frame: mcid_rr=0.59 encodes a clinically meaningful benefit in the observed (caff-favours) direction; design_hr=1/0.59=1.695 encodes the opposite pre-specified direction. The Design-informed prior therefore CONFLICTS with the observed effect - this is the DECAF 'twist'. e_t/e_c not extractable from the public summary; PMID is placeholder.
Sacubitril/valsartan vs enalapril; powered for 15% RRR -> mcid_rr=design_hr=0.85. Design and observed are in the same direction (benefit).
Empagliflozin vs placebo in T2DM with CVD; designed for non-inferiority then superiority; mcid_rr=design_hr=0.82 (~18% RRR target).
Dapagliflozin in HFrEF; powered for 20% RRR -> mcid_rr=design_hr=0.80.

endpoint: CV death, MI, HF hosp, UA, resuscitated cardiac arrest
notes: Invasive vs conservative strategy; designed to detect 18.5% RRR.

Primary outcome is mean difference in exercise-time seconds (PCI vs sham); kept in the cohort for descriptive purposes and skipped by the HR-based Bayesian pipeline. mcid_rr/design_hr are nominal placeholders used only for plotting.
Empagliflozin in HFrEF; used to fill the 'JAMA Cardiology / SGLT2i' slot. EMPEROR-Reduced was actually published in NEJM; journal field reflects the real publication venue.

endpoint: CV death, nonfatal MI, nonfatal stroke
notes: Semaglutide 2.4 mg in overweight/obesity with CVD (no diabetes); used here to round out the cohort. SELECT was published in NEJM; note the journal field.
